message:
fix(database/mysql): refresh in_transaction status flag from result-set terminator

The MySqlConnection.status_flags field was previously only updated by
the explicit OK packet path during query() / execute() / ping().  When
the server used the DEPRECATE_EOF capability, result sets terminated
with an EOF-shaped (0xFE) OK packet rather than a regular 0x00 OK
packet, and that terminator was being routed through parse_ok_packet()
which then refused to parse it (since the new strict parser only
accepts 0x00 — see below).  Status flags were therefore stuck at the
handshake values, and in_transaction() returned stale results
immediately after a SELECT inside a transaction.

Changes:

- Hoist DEFAULT_MAX_RESULT_ROWS, MAX_COLUMN_COUNT, and
  MAX_REASSEMBLED_PACKET_SIZE to module-scope constants so they are
  visible across all the helper functions and not redeclared inside
  hot loops.

- Extract push_result_row() so the row-loop body is small and the
  terminator branch can early-break without nesting.

- Add parse_result_set_terminator_status_flags(): the single helper
  used by the result-set drain to read status flags from either an
  EOF packet (legacy protocol) or an OK-shaped (0x00 / 0xFE) packet
  (DEPRECATE_EOF protocol).

- Add parse_ok_packet_like_status_flags(): the lenient form that
  accepts both 0x00 and 0xFE OK-shaped packets.  Used only from
  parse_result_set_terminator_status_flags().

- Tighten parse_ok_packet() to strictly require a 0x00 first byte.
  This ensures command response parsing cannot accidentally consume
  an EOF-terminator-shaped packet, preserving the protocol contract
  documented in the MySQL wire protocol.

- Replace the in-loop ad-hoc EOF/OK demux at the end of the result
  set with a single call to parse_result_set_terminator_status_flags,
  ensuring status flags are always refreshed from whichever terminator
  the server actually sent.

- Add deprecate_eof_ok_packet_payload() test fixture and
  query_deprecate_eof_ok_terminator_updates_in_transaction_status_flag
  regression test that:
  1. Stands up a TCP test server speaking DEPRECATE_EOF protocol
  2. Sends a column-count + column-def + 0xFE OK-shaped terminator
     with SERVER_STATUS_IN_TRANS bit set
  3. Asserts conn.in_transaction() returns true after the query

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
